title: ZOO REVIEW 3- San Francisco Zoo - The WOLFe Pack
content: # ZOO REVIEW 3- San Francisco Zoo. Animals are our son’s passion and at 12-years-old, he insists he wants to be a professional zookeeper when he grows up. We had three days scheduled in San Francisco and, considering the number of fabulous choices of fun in the area,  the zoo was NOT on my top list of things to do. But my husband and son argued for it and I’m really glad we made it work. We actually managed to thoroughly explore the zoo and still had time in the day to check into our downtown hotel and then take a trolley down to the wharf for some sea lion fun, a yummy dinner and Ghirardelli sundaes for dessert. We experienced a little confusion due to the current state of their expansion….not in the sense of detours and construction but in the sense of “is this exhibit complete or are they still working on it?

content: Their distinctive features and elegance are sure to catch your attention, even if the viewing area may occasionally be closed to the public.

title: Great way to spend a half day - Review of San Francisco Zoo, San ...
content: # Great way to spend a half day - San Francisco Zoo. *   San Francisco - Things to Do. **#71** of 1,166 things to do in San Francisco. # Great way to spend a half day. Review of San Francisco Zoo. In terms of Zoo's in CA, I have to say I this could be my favorite. It was my first visit here and had only expected to spend a couple of hours and ended up being there until it nearly closed. One item that definitely stood out to me was the large size of many of the animal enclosures. Now, I am neither an ecologist or biologist with a specialty in animal habitat, but all the animals seemed to have plenty of land to roam free in, even if they chose not to. I really loved how the it gave the zoo that established presence without feeling like the it was outdated and in need of rennovations.

title: Reviews of San Francisco zoo? - Facebook
content: Don't bother. Often foggy. Great for little kiddies. Way better zoos elsewhere like San Diego. Even Oakland's is much better.
